subject_tmpl: Tall weeds at {ADDRESS} — please send someone out
body_tmpl: Hi, my name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I live near {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. The grass and weeds in that yard are easily two feet tall, maybe more. It's been like this for weeks and no one seems to care. This is a nuisance for the whole block — bugs, rodents, you name it. Can the Village please send an inspector out there? I'd appreciate any update you can give me. Thanks.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thank you for contacting us. We've opened case {CASE} for the property at {ADDRESS}. An inspector will visit within {DAYS} business days and the property owner will receive a notice. You can call our office at any time to check status.

subject_tmpl: Overgrown lot on {STREET} — this has gone on too long
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I am writing because the vacant lot at {ADDRESS} has not been mowed all season. The weeds are chest-high and the alley behind it is filled with seeds and debris. I called the code office on {DATE} but nothing happened. Neighbors on our block are frustrated. I'm asking you personally to intervene and get this cleaned up before the Fourth of July weekend. Thank you.

subject_tmpl: Junk car sitting in driveway at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} here, from {STREET}. There's an old {AGE}-year-old car at {ADDRESS} that hasn't moved in months. Flat tires, no plates, busted windshield. It's an eyesore and my kids walk past it every day on the way to school. Is there an ordinance that covers this? Please look into it.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, we've logged your concern as case {CASE}. Inoperable vehicles on private property are subject to Village code. An officer will inspect by {DATE} and issue a notice if warranted.

subject_tmpl: Multiple inoperable vehicles — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Good morning. I want to report what looks like a small junkyard forming at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. I count at least three cars with no plates, one up on blocks, and a trailer with a missing axle. This has been building up since {DATE}. The owner doesn't respond to neighbors. Please advise what steps the Village can take.

subject_tmpl: Foreclosed home at {ADDRESS} — total mess
body_tmpl: Hi Sandra, I know you've probably heard about {ADDRESS} already. The bank took it over last year and nobody is maintaining it. Broken windows boarded up with plywood, gutters hanging off, weeds everywhere. It's dragging down property values on {STREET}. Who do we contact to force the lender to keep it up? Any help is appreciated. — {NAME}
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, we have an active file on {ADDRESS}. We've sent notices to the mortgage servicer under our vacant property registration ordinance. The servicer has {DAYS} days to respond. I'll follow up with you once we hear back.

subject_tmpl: Abandoned foreclosure on {STREET} is a safety hazard
body_tmpl: Mayor, the property at {ADDRESS} has been abandoned for over {DAYS} days. Kids have broken in twice. There is graffiti on the garage and the rear fence is completely down. I've called code enforcement and public works. Nothing is happening fast enough. This is a real safety issue, especially with children in the neighborhood. Please make this a priority. Respectfully, {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Illegal dumping in the alley behind {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hello. Someone has been dumping old furniture, tires, and black garbage bags in the alley between {STREET} and {CROSS}. It started with a mattress on {DATE} and there's more junk every few days now. The alley is almost impassable. Please send someone to clean it up and put up a no-dumping sign. Thank you. — {FIRST}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, we've assigned case {CASE} and alerted Public Works to schedule a cleanup. We'll also check alley camera footage. The cleanup is scheduled for within {DAYS} days.

subject_tmpl: Construction debris dumped in alley off {STREET}
body_tmpl: Good afternoon. There is a large pile of drywall, lumber scraps, and concrete rubble dumped in the alley behind {ADDRESS}. It looks like contractor debris. The pile is easily five feet high. My trash hauler couldn't even get through this morning. This needs to be cleaned up immediately and whoever did this should be fined.

subject_tmpl: Car parked on front lawn at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I live across the street from {ADDRESS}. My neighbor has been parking two or three cars on the grass in front of their house since {DATE}. The lawn is now completely destroyed — just mud ruts. I believe the Village has a rule against parking on unpaved surfaces. Can someone please check on this? My contact number is {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out, {FIRST}. Parking on unpaved areas violates Village ordinance. We've created case {CASE} and will inspect {ADDRESS} within {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Ongoing lawn parking issue on {STREET} — no action taken
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I reported lawn parking at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} (case {CASE}) and again two weeks later. Both times I was told an inspector would come. The cars are still there. My property value is suffering. I pay my taxes and I expect the Village to enforce its own rules. I'm asking you to look into why nothing has been done. Thank you. {NAME}

subject_tmpl: Weeds along fence line — {ADDRESS} on {STREET}
body_tmpl: {NAME} calling in by email. The fence line along the east side of {ADDRESS} has been overtaken by tall weeds — pokeweed, thistle, the whole works. They're spreading seeds into my vegetable garden. The property is a rental and the landlord never maintains it. Can you send code enforcement to issue a citation?

subject_tmpl: Please help — rat problem tracing back to dumped garbage at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi, my family lives next door to {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. Since garbage and appliances were dumped behind that property, we've been seeing rats in our yard and even our garage. I set traps but the source of the problem is next door. I need code enforcement and maybe the health department to get involved. This is a health issue. Please respond. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, we take sanitation and rodent concerns very seriously. We've forwarded your complaint to both code enforcement and our health officer. Expect contact within {DAYS} business days. Case reference: {CASE}.

subject_tmpl: Alley cleanup request — debris at {ADDRESS}, case {CASE}
body_tmpl: Frank, we've confirmed illegal dumping in the alley behind {ADDRESS}. The pile includes a sofa, two mattresses, and multiple bags of household trash. Resident complaint received {DATE}. Can your crew schedule a pickup? Once cleared, I'll follow up on any camera footage we might have for enforcement. Let me know the expected date. — Sandra
reply_tmpl: Sandra, we can get a crew out there on {DATE}. I'll have them document it with photos before clearing. Loop in Reggie if you want a police report filed for dumping enforcement.

subject_tmpl: Rodent complaint linked to dumping — {ADDRESS} — need joint inspection
body_tmpl: Priya, we have a code case ({CASE}) for illegal dumping at {ADDRESS} that a neighbor says is sourcing a rat problem. I'm scheduling my inspection for {DATE}. Would you be able to join or do a follow-up health inspection the same week? I think a joint visit makes a stronger record if we need to escalate to a hearing. Thanks, Sandra.

subject_tmpl: Escalation — repeat violation at {ADDRESS}, {STREET}
body_tmpl: Karen, I'm flagging {ADDRESS} for your awareness. We've cited this property three times in the past {DAYS} days for tall weeds and an inoperable vehicle. The owner has not complied and the latest hearing date was ignored. I'm recommending we proceed with administrative adjudication and seek authorization for Village contractor mowing at owner's expense. Please advise how you'd like to handle the communication to the Mayor. — Sandra Pulaski
reply_tmpl: Sandra, agreed on adjudication. Draft the hearing notice and copy finance so we have a lien mechanism ready. I'll brief the Mayor. Thanks for staying on this.

subject_tmpl: Tall grass at corner of {STREET} and {CROSS}
body_tmpl: To whom it may concern: The vacant corner lot at {STREET} and {CROSS} looks terrible. Grass is well over a foot tall and there are weeds growing through the sidewalk cracks. I walk my dog there every morning and it's getting hard to even navigate the sidewalk. Who owns this lot and when can we expect it to be mowed? Thanks, {NAME}.

subject_tmpl: Why is nothing being done about {ADDRESS}?
body_tmpl: Mayor, I have emailed the Village three times about the property at {ADDRESS}. Each time I'm told the case is open. But I drove by this morning and nothing has changed. Tall weeds, a junk car, trash piled in the driveway. My family has lived on {STREET} for {AGE} years and I've never seen the block look this bad. Please tell me someone is actually working on this. Very frustrated. {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Inoperable van — {ADDRESS} — been there {DAYS} days
body_tmpl: Good evening. I'm writing about a white van parked in front of {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. It's been sitting there for {DAYS} days, no movement, flat tires, and the registration sticker is from two years ago. The whole neighborhood can see it. Please issue a citation or have it towed. I'd rather not have to call the police if the Village can handle it. Thank you, {FIRST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, our officer will inspect {ADDRESS} on {DATE}. If the vehicle meets the criteria for inoperability, a notice will be issued with a {DAYS}-day compliance window. We'll be in touch.

subject_tmpl: Foreclosure on {STREET} — windows broken, squatters?
body_tmpl: Hi. The house at {ADDRESS} has been sitting empty since at least {DATE}. A couple of the windows are broken. Last weekend I heard people inside at night — could be squatters. I'm worried about fire risk. Can code enforcement and maybe the police do a walk-around? I'm happy to talk to someone in person. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you {FIRST}. We've flagged this for a joint code and police walkthrough this week. We'll also check our vacant property registry. Appreciate you looking out for the neighborhood.

subject_tmpl: Dumping — tires and old appliances, alley on {CROSS}
body_tmpl: Hello, someone dropped four used tires and a broken dishwasher in the alley between {STREET} and {CROSS} sometime on {DATE}. I took photos if that's helpful. I've seen the same truck in the alley before — dark pickup, no plates visible. Can the Village arrange removal? And is there a reward for reporting illegal dumpers? Thanks, {NAME}.

subject_tmpl: Cars on lawn destroying the parkway — {STREET}
body_tmpl: The residents at {ADDRESS} have been driving across the parkway to park in their backyard. There's now a set of deep tire ruts across the grass and through the parkway tree roots. I'm worried about the tree. Does the Village consider parkway damage a code issue or a public works issue? Either way it needs to be fixed. My name is {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, this crosses both departments. We'll open a code case for the unpaved parking and loop in Public Works to assess the parkway and tree. Thanks for flagging the tree — that's important.

subject_tmpl: Weed overgrowth blocking alley visibility at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi Sandra, it's {NAME} again from {STREET}. The weeds along the back of {ADDRESS} have grown so tall they're blocking line-of-sight when you pull out of the alley onto {CROSS}. I almost got hit last week. This is now a safety concern, not just a nuisance. Please treat this as urgent.

subject_tmpl: Three junk vehicles at {ADDRESS} — request for action
body_tmpl: Good morning. I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {STREET}. The property at {ADDRESS} has three vehicles I'd describe as inoperable: a sedan with no engine (hood always propped open), a rusted-out pickup, and a boat on a flat trailer. None appear registered. The owner is rarely home. I tried speaking with them but got nowhere. Please open a case and let me know what happens.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, case {CASE} is now open for {ADDRESS}. An inspector will visit within {DAYS} business days. We'll document all three vehicles and issue notices as appropriate.

subject_tmpl: Thank you for cleaning up {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to send a quick note of thanks. The Village finally cleaned up the lot at {ADDRESS} that I'd been reporting since {DATE}. The weeds are gone, the junk car is gone, and the alley looks so much better. I know it takes time and I appreciate that someone followed through. You have a great team. Sincerely, {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Thank you — {ADDRESS} is finally cleaned up
body_tmpl: Sandra, just a quick thank-you email. The property at {ADDRESS} that I reported on {DATE} has been cleaned up. The weeds are cut, the old car is gone, and the property actually looks decent now. I know you hear a lot of complaints so I wanted to make sure I also said when things go right. Really appreciate your work. — {NAME}
reply_tmpl: Thank you so much, {NAME}! Feedback like this means a lot to our team. We're glad the block is looking better. Don't hesitate to reach out if anything else comes up.

subject_tmpl: Lien authorization request — {ADDRESS}, case {CASE}
body_tmpl: David, we need to move forward with a special assessment lien against {ADDRESS}. The Village mowed the property on {DATE} after the owner failed to comply for {DAYS} days. Total cost was {AMOUNT}. Please confirm the lien can be filed against the property account and advise on the process for a Cook County lien attachment. Thank you. — Sandra Pulaski, Code Enforcement
reply_tmpl: Sandra, confirmed. Send me the final invoice and the legal description and I'll get the lien filed within the week. Copy the clerk's office so the record is complete.

subject_tmpl: Overgrown bushes from {ADDRESS} blocking the sidewalk
body_tmpl: Hi there. The shrubs and bushes along the front of {ADDRESS} on {STREET} have grown out over the sidewalk — you have to step into the street to get by. For someone using a stroller or wheelchair it's impossible. I'd love it if the Village could require the homeowner to trim them back. Thank you for your time. {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Illegal auto repair going on at {ADDRESS} every weekend
body_tmpl: Code enforcement team, I need to report that the resident at {ADDRESS} has been doing commercial-style auto repair in their driveway and on the street every Saturday and Sunday. Multiple cars brought in by strangers, oil stains on the road, parts everywhere. Cars parked all over the lawn too. This is a residential street and it's becoming a junkyard on weekends. Can you please visit on a {DATE} morning?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for the report. Home-based auto repair businesses require a business license and must comply with zoning. We'll schedule a weekend inspection for {DATE} and document the activity. Reference case {CASE}.

subject_tmpl: Abandoned camper parked on lawn — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Good afternoon. A large camper trailer has been sitting on the lawn of {ADDRESS} since at least {DATE}. It's not on a paved surface, the wheels are sinking into the ground, and it blocks most of the front yard. Neighbors are not happy. Please advise. {NAME}, {STREET}.

subject_tmpl: Dumping behind the plaza on {STREET} — ongoing problem
body_tmpl: Hello. The alley behind the strip mall on {STREET} near {CROSS} has become a dump site. People leave mattresses, broken TVs, bags of garbage — all behind the dumpsters that aren't theirs to use. It has been going on for months. The plaza owners don't seem to care. Can the Village issue a warning or fine to the property owner for not securing the site? — {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Complaint about dumping behind {BIZ} — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm the manager at {BIZ} located at {ADDRESS}. Someone has been dumping bags of trash behind our building in the alley — not in our dumpster, just on the ground. We've cleaned it up twice but it keeps coming back. Can the Village help us figure out who's doing this and what our options are for preventing it? We'd like to install a camera but wanted to check if permits are needed. Thanks.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out. No permit is required for a private security camera on your own property. We've logged a dumping complaint (case {CASE}) and will check the alley. We can also look at whether additional 'No Dumping' signage is warranted.

subject_tmpl: Snow still not cleared — weeds underneath from last summer
body_tmpl: I know it's winter but I'm writing ahead of spring. The vacant lot at {ADDRESS} was never mowed last summer and the tall dead weeds are still standing under the snow. Once things thaw that lot is going to be a disaster again. Can code enforcement put a note to proactively inspect {ADDRESS} in April? I don't want to go through the same back-and-forth we had last year.

subject_tmpl: Junk piled in side yard visible from {STREET}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} here. The side yard at {ADDRESS} is full of junk — old bicycles, broken furniture, scrap metal, garden hoses, bags of who-knows-what. It's clearly visible from {STREET} and it's been there for well over {DAYS} days. Does this fall under the exterior property maintenance code? I'd like to file a formal complaint if possible.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, yes this falls under our exterior property maintenance code. We've opened case {CASE} for {ADDRESS}. The owner will receive notice with {DAYS} days to comply. Thank you.

subject_tmpl: Foreclosure property — no one cutting the grass since {DATE}
body_tmpl: Hello. The bank-owned property at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} has not been mowed since {DATE}. I've watched the weeds go from normal to nearly three feet tall. I called the number on the sign in the window and left two messages with no response. Is there a way the Village can step in and bill the bank? That's the only language they seem to understand.

subject_tmpl: Request for police assist — possible squatters at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Reggie, I have a code complaint for {ADDRESS} — it's a foreclosed property and a resident reports possible squatters inside. Before I do my exterior inspection I'd like a quick welfare/safety check from your end. No need for a full response, just a walk-around on {DATE} before I show up. Let me know if that works. — Sandra
reply_tmpl: Sandra, I'll have someone swing by in the morning on {DATE}. I'll text you with the all-clear before you go in. If we find anything, we'll coordinate from there.

subject_tmpl: Could you look at this before our block club meeting?
body_tmpl: Hi Sandra. Our block club on {STREET} meets on {DATE} at {TIME}. One of the top concerns is the property at {ADDRESS} — junk cars and tall grass all year long. Is it possible for someone from code enforcement to attend or at least give me an update I can share with the group? We have about {NUM} households that have been watching this situation for months.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, I can give you a status update to share with the group. Case {CASE} is open and we're in the notice period. I can't make the meeting but feel free to share this email. I'll try to have more progress to report by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Lawn parking every night — {STREET} near {CROSS}
body_tmpl: Team, I want to report that the household at {ADDRESS} parks vehicles on the lawn nightly. By morning there are usually two trucks and a car sitting on what used to be grass. The front yard is now mud. This seems to be a chronic issue with no enforcement. I have photos from the past {DAYS} days. Can I submit them to support a case?
reply_tmpl: Yes, please send your photos to this email address. Visual documentation is very helpful for our enforcement record. We've opened case {CASE} and will schedule a visit soon.

subject_tmpl: Burned-out car sitting at {ADDRESS} for {DAYS} days
body_tmpl: Good morning. A car that appears to have been burned or badly damaged is sitting in the driveway at {ADDRESS}. It's been there for {DAYS} days. There's charring on the body and no glass left. Kids walk by every day on their way to school and it's alarming to look at. Please advise on next steps. — {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Weeds coming through fence from {ADDRESS} onto my property
body_tmpl: Hi. My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {STREET}. My neighbor at {ADDRESS} has let their back yard go completely wild. Vines and weeds are actually growing through and over the fence onto my side. I've trimmed my side but the source of the problem is their yard. Can code enforcement issue a notice requiring them to clear their back yard? Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, we'll include the rear yard in our inspection of {ADDRESS} under case {CASE}. Vegetation encroaching on neighboring property is a code violation. We'll let you know the outcome.

subject_tmpl: I need to know: what happens after a code notice is issued?
body_tmpl: Hello. I filed a complaint about {ADDRESS} on {DATE} and I was told a notice would be mailed to the owner. It's been {DAYS} days and nothing looks different. Can you explain what the timeline is after a notice goes out? What are the next steps if the owner ignores it? I just want to understand the process. Thank you, {NAME}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, after the notice the owner has {DAYS} days to comply. If they don't, we schedule a reinspection and can issue a fine or summons to an administrative hearing. The hearing officer can impose fines and authorize the Village to abate and lien. We'll send you an update once we reinspect {ADDRESS}.

subject_tmpl: Alley dumping near {ADDRESS} — third time this month
body_tmpl: This is the third time I've written about dumping in the alley near {ADDRESS}. Every few days there's new garbage. Last week it was bags of drywall dust. This week it's old carpet. I understand it's a persistent problem but I feel like the responses I get are just form letters. Can someone actually tell me what is being done to stop this? {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Front yard used as storage lot — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Good afternoon. I live near {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. The front yard is being used as a storage area — appliances, pallets, lumber, boxes of who knows what, and two cars that don't run. It looks like a warehouse yard, not a home. I've tolerated it for {DAYS} days but it's getting worse, not better. Please look into it. {NAME}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you {NAME}. We'll inspect {ADDRESS} under our exterior storage and inoperable vehicle ordinances. Expect a response within {DAYS} business days after inspection.

subject_tmpl: Very high weeds — {ADDRESS} — I'm an elderly neighbor and can't mow for them
body_tmpl: Hello. My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I'm {AGE} years old, living on {STREET}. My next-door neighbor at {ADDRESS} is renting and the landlord never sends anyone to mow. The weeds are over my head in places. I can barely manage my own yard and I certainly can't deal with theirs too. I'm asking the Village to please do something. God bless you.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you for writing. We opened case {CASE} for {ADDRESS} today. An inspector will visit this week and the landlord will be contacted directly. We appreciate you reaching out and we'll take care of this.

subject_tmpl: FOIA request — code enforcement records for {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello. I am requesting under FOIA all code enforcement complaints, inspection reports, notices, and citations for the property at {ADDRESS} for the period {DATE} through the present. I am considering purchasing this property and want to understand its compliance history. Please let me know the fee, if any, and the estimated turnaround time. Thank you. {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your FOIA request. We've assigned it reference number {CASE}. The response deadline is {DATE} per Illinois law. If records involve fees over {AMOUNT}, we'll notify you in advance. — Yolanda Pierce, Village Clerk

subject_tmpl: Property owner mowing at {TIME} — disturbing neighbors
body_tmpl: Hi. I actually support the code enforcement notices about {ADDRESS} — the owner finally started mowing. But he mows at {TIME} on Sunday mornings with an extremely loud riding mower. Is there a Village noise ordinance that restricts lawn equipment hours? I'd like to sleep past 7 a.m. on weekends. — {NAME}
